Name: Lenny's Podcast
Description: In-depth interviews with world-class product leaders, founders, and growth experts. Tactical, actionable advice on building, launching, and scaling products and careers. The most popular PM podcast.

Name: Product Thinking
Description: Melissa Perri (author of Escaping the Build Trap) talks with product leaders and answers listener "Dear Melissa" questions. Covers strategy, org design, product operations, and the realities of leading product teams.

Name: The Product Experience
Description: Mind the Product's official podcast. Practitioner-focused conversations with product leaders on discovery, research, strategy, AI, and team dynamics. Grounded, no-fluff interviews from the global product community.

Name: The Product Podcast
Description: Product School's flagship show. Interviews with Chief Product Officers and product executives from companies like Shopify, Cisco, and Perplexity on leadership, frameworks, and building products in the age of AI.

Name: One Knight in Product
Description: Jason Knight interviews product leaders, founders, operators, and authors about building, scaling, and leading great products. Wide-ranging conversations on management, leadership, careers, and the realities of the craft.

Name: Productside Stories
Description: Honest conversations with experienced product leaders, hosted by the Productside training and consulting team. Covers product management practice, leadership lessons, and how PMs use AI as a thought partner.

Name: ProductLed Podcast
Description: Wes Bush interviews product-led growth leaders with hands-on knowledge of using the product itself to drive business growth. Tactical lessons on PLG, onboarding, monetization, and scaling.

Name: The Product Manager
Description: A 360-degree view of product through the perspectives of those inside and around the product process. Candid, actionable insights spanning every stage of the product lifecycle.

Name: Intercom on Product (/ideas)
Description: Intercom's senior leaders discuss product strategy, building software, and scaling product organizations, reframed around building in an AI-first world. Deep, opinionated conversations on craft and decision-making.

Name: Product Chats
Description: Pragmatic Institute's podcast tackling the biggest challenges in product management and product marketing. Market-driven, data-informed conversations with practitioners and product leaders.

Name: Product for Product Management
Description: A journey through the tools and categories that make up the PM role — analytics, roadmapping, productivity, and more. Interviews with makers and practitioners about the product management toolkit and craft.

Name: The Product Science Podcast
Description: Holly Hester-Reilly invites product leaders to present a hypothesis about how great products are built, then digs into it. A scientific, evidence-driven lens on product development and discovery.

Name: Product Talk (Products That Count)
Description: Products That Count's podcast featuring CPOs and product leaders. The "CPO Rising" series and guest interviews cover product leadership, strategy, and how top executives build and scale products.

Name: The Growth Podcast (Product Growth)
Description: Aakash Gupta interviews PM and growth experts, with a heavy focus on AI product management and the evolving PM role. Twice-weekly tactical conversations; some bonus content is paid.

Name: Product Momentum
Description: ITX's podcast featuring the minds shaping the product industry. A marketplace of ideas on product management, design, research, and innovation, with practitioner and thought-leader interviews.

Name: Practical Product Management
Description: Two veterans with 20+ years at Amazon and Expedia peel back PM theory to focus on real decision-making in high-stakes tech environments. Practical, experience-driven conversations on the nuts and bolts of the role.

Name: Product Mastery Now
Description: Chad McAllister interviews product managers, leaders, and innovators on product management and new product development. Long-running show (590+ episodes) on frameworks, innovation, and professional growth.

Name: Behind the Craft
Description: Peter Yang explores how top builders craft products, with a strong focus on AI-native product development, founders, and shipping fast. Practical conversations on product craft, strategy, and using AI in the PM workflow.

Name: The Skip Podcast
Description: Three-time founder and Meta/Google CPO Nikhyl Singhal coaches tech professionals on getting ahead. Real coaching conversations on product leadership, management challenges, and career transitions.

Name: All Things Product (Teresa & Petra)
Description: Continuous-discovery pioneer Teresa Torres and product coach Petra Wille trade short, no-fluff takes on the challenges, trends, and ideas shaping how we build products. 15-30 minute conversational episodes.

Name: The Intentional Product Manager
Description: Ex-Google PM Shobhit Chugh helps ambitious product managers position themselves to become product leaders. Career-focused conversations on impact, influence, and growing into product leadership.

Name: Lessons in Product Management
Description: Path2Product's show bridging the experience gap for aspiring PMs while offering insights to existing PMs and leaders. Practical lessons on breaking into and growing within product management.

Name: The Product Huddle
Description: A podcast for product managers, leaders, and founders who want to level up their impact. Real-world conversations on product strategy, leadership, decision-making, and the invisible work of the PM role.

Name: My First Million
Description: Two operators riff on business models, side hustles, and acquisition opportunities, often reverse-engineering how companies make money. High signal for PMs who want to think in markets, monetization, and zero-to-one bets.

Name: The Twenty Minute VC (20VC)
Description: Interviews with top VCs, founders, and operators on fundraising, company building, and market strategy. Tight, tactical conversations that help PMs understand how investors and founders evaluate products and markets.

Name: How I Built This
Description: Narrative interviews with founders of iconic companies on the messy origin stories behind their products. Great for PMs studying product-market fit, persistence, and pivots through a storytelling lens.

Name: Masters of Scale
Description: Iconic business leaders unpack the counterintuitive theories behind scaling companies. Strong on growth strategy, network effects, and leadership decisions for PMs aspiring to founder-thinking.

Name: This Week in Startups
Description: News, founder interviews, and investor commentary on the startup ecosystem, fundraising climate, and emerging trends. Keeps PMs current on what founders and VCs are actually doing right now.

Name: Startups For the Rest of Us
Description: No-fluff tactical advice on bootstrapping and scaling SaaS without VC. Excellent for PMs who want founder-level ownership of growth, pricing, and product decisions in lean teams.

Name: The a16z Podcast
Description: a16z partners and guests explore tech trends, AI, and how software reshapes industries. Helps PMs build market intuition and frameworks for where products are heading. Thesis-driven and forward-looking.

Name: The Official SaaStr Podcast
Description: Interviews with SaaS operators and investors on go-to-market, scaling ARR, and building software companies. Directly relevant to B2B PMs thinking about growth, pricing, and GTM. Tactical and metrics-heavy.

Name: Y Combinator Startup Podcast
Description: YC partners and founders share startup advice on building, fundraising, and finding product-market fit. Distilled YC wisdom that helps PMs think like early-stage founders. Practical and no-nonsense.

Name: Lightcone
Host(s): Garry Tan, Diana Hu, Harj Taggar & Jared Friedman
Description: YC's top partners break down the newest tech, AI startup ideas, and what they're seeing across batches. High-signal for PMs hunting where new product opportunities are emerging.

Name: Invest Like the Best
Description: Long-form interviews with top investors, founders, and operators on markets, strategy, and the craft of building and allocating capital. Surfaces mental models and business insight for strategic thinkers.

Name: The Knowledge Project
Description: Interviews on decision-making, mental models, and how top performers think. Sharpens the judgment and reasoning PMs need to make better product and strategy calls. Reflective and framework-rich.

Name: All-In
Host(s): Chamath Palihapitiya, Jason Calacanis, David Sacks & David Friedberg
Description: Four investor-operators debate tech, markets, economics, and politics. Useful for PMs who want macro and market context behind product and company decisions. Fast, candid, and opinionated.

Name: The Pitch
Description: Real founders pitch real VCs, with the negotiation and feedback captured live. Lets PMs see how investors evaluate products, traction, and founders in real time. Dramatic and instructive.

Name: Grit
Description: Kleiner Perkins' Joubin interviews top tech leaders on resilience, leadership, and the craft of building companies. Strong for PMs studying operator mindset and go-to-market scaling.

Name: The Diary of a CEO
Description: Long-form interviews with founders, CEOs, and high performers on business, psychology, and personal growth. Helps PMs absorb leadership and resilience lessons from builders.

Name: BG2 Pod
Description: Two heavyweight investors talk tech, markets, AI, and capitalism with unusual depth and candor. Strong on platform shifts, valuations, and AI-era business strategy.

Name: In Depth
Description: First Round's Brett Berson surfaces tactical advice from founders and startup leaders on building teams, products, and companies. Dense with frameworks PMs can apply to scaling and management.

Name: The Generalist
Description: Interviews and analysis from the writer behind The Generalist on tech, venture, and the companies and founders shaping the future. Strategy-forward conversations with operators and investors.

Name: Cheeky Pint
Description: Stripe co-founder John Collison hosts founders and leaders for candid conversations over a pint. Operator-to-operator talk on company-building, strategy, and scaling, with notable guests.

Name: Latent Space
Description: The definitive podcast for AI engineers, going deep on the tools, models, and architectures behind production AI products. Interviews with founders, researchers, and builders shipping real LLM applications.

Name: No Priors
Description: Two prominent investors interview the founders and researchers shaping AI's frontier. Sharp conversations on where the technology and market are heading. Great for PMs tracking strategy and emerging product categories.

Name: The Cognitive Revolution
Description: Deep, builder-oriented interviews with people on AI's cutting edge, often hands-on with new models and tools. A practitioner's lens on capabilities and implications for PMs.

Name: Practical AI
Description: Approachable conversations making AI/ML practical for working teams. Covers tooling, MLOps, LLMs, and real deployment patterns. Strong fit for PMs who want grounded, applied takes rather than hype.

Name: The TWIML AI Podcast
Description: Long-running, technically rigorous interviews with ML researchers and practitioners across the breadth of machine learning, from research to production. A staple for PMs who want depth on models and methods.

Name: Dwarkesh Podcast
Description: Unusually deep, well-researched long-form interviews with AI researchers, scientists, economists, and thinkers. Known for rigorous, probing questions on AGI, scaling, and progress. First-principles and demanding.

Name: Gradient Dissent
Description: W&B's show featuring ML leaders on how they build and deploy AI in production. Practical conversations on real-world ML engineering challenges. Good for PMs partnering closely with ML teams.

Name: Hard Fork
Description: A lively weekly take on the tech news that matters, with heavy AI coverage. Smart, accessible analysis of industry moves, products, and policy. Great for the broader tech-culture context around AI.

Name: AI + a16z
Description: a16z's AI-focused show with founders and technical leaders building the AI stack — infrastructure, models, agents, and go-to-market. Useful for PMs tracking where AI investment and product opportunity are heading.

Name: Lex Fridman Podcast
Description: Marathon-length conversations with leading AI researchers, founders, and scientists alongside broader topics. PMs can cherry-pick frontier-AI episodes for big-picture understanding.

Name: The AI Daily Brief
Description: A daily briefing on the most important AI news and analysis, framed for people building and deploying AI. Fast, opinionated synthesis of the day's developments in 10-20 minutes.

Name: Last Week in AI
Description: A weekly roundup digesting the major AI research, products, and policy news. Thorough and technical without being inaccessible. Good for a comprehensive catch-up on the week.

Name: Machine Learning Street Talk
Description: Deep, intellectually demanding discussions on ML theory, reasoning, and the frontiers of AI research, with top researchers. Best for PMs who want to stretch into the conceptual underpinnings.

Name: Eye on A.I.
Description: Measured, interview-driven exploration of AI's progress and impact with researchers and executives. Balanced, less hype-driven coverage of where the field is genuinely heading.

Name: Training Data
Description: Sequoia's show unpacking the AI breakthroughs and companies defining the era, with founders and researchers. Strong on the intersection of technology and market for product strategy and category creation.

Name: Unsupervised Learning
Description: Redpoint's interviews with founders and operators building AI products and companies. Practical conversations on product, GTM, and technical decisions. Highly relevant for PMs building in AI.

Name: This Day in AI
Description: A candid, builder-flavored weekly look at the latest AI news, models, and experiments, with two technical hosts reacting to and testing new releases. Entertaining and useful for tracking practical capabilities.

Name: The Vergecast
Host(s): Nilay Patel, David Pierce & Alex Cranz
Description: The Verge's flagship show on the week in consumer tech, with substantial AI product coverage. Sharp, accessible takes on gadgets, platforms, and policy — good for consumer-tech and product-design context.

Name: Decoder with Nilay Patel
Description: In-depth interviews with leaders on the big strategic and organizational decisions behind tech and AI products. Focused on how decisions get made and businesses run. Excellent for strategy and org design.

Name: The MAD Podcast
Description: Conversations on the Machine Learning, AI, and Data (MAD) landscape with founders and researchers. Maps the rapidly evolving data and AI infrastructure ecosystem. Useful for navigating the tooling and data stack.

Name: ThursdAI
Description: A weekly recap of every meaningful AI release, often recorded live with practitioners. Fast-moving, builder-centric coverage of models, tools, and open source. Great for the practitioner's pulse on what shipped.

Name: Big Technology Podcast
Description: Interviews and news analysis on big tech and AI, balancing optimism with scrutiny, with executives, founders, and journalists. Good for strategic industry context with a critical edge.

Name: Possible
Description: Explores how AI and technology could build a better future, with builders, researchers, and leaders. Optimistic but substantive, scenario-driven conversations useful for long-term product vision.

Name: Google DeepMind: The Podcast
Description: DeepMind's own show explaining frontier AI research in accessible terms, hosted by Hannah Fry. Clear walkthroughs of breakthroughs from the people building them. Credible, digestible research context.

Name: Mixture of Experts
Description: IBM's weekly panel debating the biggest AI news with rotating engineers and researchers. Practitioner-grounded discussion of models, agents, and enterprise AI. Useful for applied and enterprise AI.

Name: Super Data Science
Description: Long-running data science and AI interviews covering careers, tools, and technical deep dives, spanning ML fundamentals to cutting-edge LLM topics. Good for building data/ML literacy.

Name: The Artificial Intelligence Show
Description: A weekly breakdown of AI news focused on what it means for businesses and how to adopt it. Practical, adoption-oriented analysis from the Marketing AI Institute for driving AI into products and orgs.

Name: Leveraging AI
Description: A business-focused show on applying AI practically inside companies and workflows, mixing news analysis with hands-on how-to guidance. Good for operationalizing AI in real products and teams.

Name: The Neuron: AI Explained
Description: An accessible show decoding AI news, tools, and trends for a broad professional audience. Quick, practical, and jargon-light — good for fast, usable AI awareness.

Name: AI Applied
Description: Short, frequent episodes on AI news, tools, and how to apply them practically across ChatGPT, Gemini, Anthropic, and the broader tool ecosystem. A steady stream of applied AI updates.

Name: The AI Engineer Podcast
Description: Talks and conversations from the AI Engineer community and conferences on building production AI systems — agents, evals, and tooling. Useful for PMs embedded with AI engineering teams.

Name: Design Better
Description: Conversations with designers, founders, and creative leaders on the intersection of design, technology, and the creative process. Pairs deep-dive interviews with practical lessons on building product and design culture.

Name: NN/g UX Podcast
Description: Nielsen Norman Group's show on UX research, design, strategy, and the profession, with expert interviews, hot takes, and evidence-based tips. A rigorous, research-grounded resource for usability and discovery.

Name: Honest UX Talks
Description: Two experienced designers share candid weekly conversations about UX careers, portfolios, design challenges, AI, and mental health. Practical and honest about the realities of working in design.

Name: Awkward Silences
Description: User Interviews' show that interviews the people who interview people, geeking out on UX research, qualitative data, and the craft of understanding users. Essential for PMs running discovery and customer research.

Name: The Futur
Description: Candid conversations exploring the overlap of design, marketing, and business with creative leaders and entrepreneurs. Focuses on making a living doing creative work, positioning, and personal brand.

Name: The Crazy One
Description: Bold insights on design, leadership, and creativity from veteran design leader Stephen Gates, mixing solo episodes and interviews on creative confidence, leading teams, and navigating AI.

Name: Finding Our Way
Description: Adaptive Path co-founders navigate the opportunities and challenges of design leadership. Thoughtful, reflective conversations on org design, design management, and the maturing of the UX profession.

Name: Design Systems Podcast (Patterns)
Description: Interviews with industry leaders on where design and development converge — design systems, tokens, and scaling product UI. Useful for PMs working on platform, component libraries, and design-dev alignment.

Name: Content Strategy Insights
Description: Weekly interviews with content strategists across UX, content design, marketing, and technical communication. Covers content modeling, governance, and structuring information for content-heavy products.

Name: UX Research Geeks
Description: UXtweak's show featuring conversations with UX research experts on methods, ops, and emerging practice. Practical and craft-focused, with a global roster of guests for deepening research literacy.

Name: User Defenders
Description: Inspiring, in-depth interviews with UX designers framed around personal growth, courage, and craft. Blends practical design advice with mindset and career development for empathy and design partnership.

Name: Design System Office Hours
Description: Practitioner-led conversations on running design systems — adoption, scaling, governance, community, and team models. Candid and hands-on, drawn from real-world systems work.

Name: Design Systems WTF
Description: zeroheight's fortnightly show where the hosts tackle a single thorny design-systems question per episode. Punchy, opinionated, and practical, with live-recorded episodes.

Name: The User Research Strategist
Description: Focused on UX research impact, strategy, and career growth, with practical guidance on demonstrating research value and influence. Short, actionable episodes for making research drive product decisions.

Name: Future of UX
Description: Explores the future of design, technology, and user experience with a strong emphasis on AI's impact on the design profession. Forward-looking interviews and solo takes on emerging tools and skills.

Name: Boagworld UX Show
Description: Long-running UK web/UX show covering UX, design leadership, conversion optimization, and digital strategy. Practical, entertaining talk-show format with frequent AI and research topics.

Name: On Theme: Design Systems in Depth
Description: In-depth conversations with design-systems practitioners on what is and isn't working in real systems — tokens, governance, and business value. For going deep on design-system strategy and ROI.

Name: Acquired
Description: Epic 3-4 hour audio documentaries dissecting the history and strategy of the world's greatest companies, from Nvidia to Hermès. Deeply researched origin stories, business-model breakdowns, and strategic playbooks.

Name: ACQ2 by Acquired
Description: The Acquired hosts' companion show: focused interviews with founders, CEOs, and investors between the main epic episodes. More conversational deep dives on company-building and strategy.

Name: Founders
Description: Solo deep dives on the lives of history's greatest entrepreneurs, drawn from biographies. Senra extracts timeless, transferable lessons on obsession, focus, and craftsmanship. Essential biographical fuel for builders.

Name: Business Breakdowns
Description: Each episode dissects a single company with an expert guest — how it makes money, its moat, unit economics, and risks. Equal parts business analysis and investing primer. A fast way to learn how businesses work.

Name: Pivot
Description: Twice-weekly takes on the biggest stories in tech, business, and politics from two sharp, contrarian voices. Fast-moving analysis of Big Tech moves, earnings, and leadership.

Name: Prof G Markets
Description: A markets-focused show breaking down the news moving stocks — earnings, Big Tech, IPOs, and macro — with Galloway's blunt frameworks plus market data and guest interviews. Accessible business-and-markets analysis.

Name: The Prof G Pod
Description: Galloway's flagship pod on business strategy, tech, marketing, and the economy, mixing solo monologues, Office Hours, and expert interviews. Brand-strategy and competitive-positioning insight.

Name: Odd Lots
Description: Bloomberg's curiosity-driven show on the most interesting corners of markets, economics, and finance. Deep, accessible explainers on everything from supply chains to market plumbing.

Name: Money Stuff: The Podcast
Description: The audio companion to Matt Levine's Money Stuff column, dissecting Wall Street, finance, and corporate absurdity with deadpan wit. Smart, funny breakdowns of deals, regulation, and market structure.

Name: Capital Allocators
Description: Interviews with the institutional investors and allocators who manage the world's largest pools of capital. Covers investment strategy, manager selection, and decision-making under uncertainty.

Name: Masters in Business
Description: Long-form interviews with the people who shape markets, investing, and business — investors, founders, and economists. Career-spanning conversations rich in strategy and biography.

Name: In Good Company
Description: The CEO of Norway's $1.7T sovereign wealth fund interviews the leaders of the world's biggest companies. Candid CEO conversations on strategy, leadership, and how giant businesses are run.

Name: The Compound and Friends
Description: Lively roundtable on markets, investing, and business with a rotating cast of finance friends. Hot takes plus genuine market analysis and data. Entertaining yet substantive markets listening.

Name: Business Movers
Description: Narrative, documentary-style retellings of the triumphs and failures behind famous companies and business leaders. Story-driven company history in the Wondery mold for biographical listening.

Name: Sharp Tech
Description: Ben Thompson applies his Stratechery frameworks to the week's tech news in conversation with Andrew Sharp — aggregation theory, platform strategy, and Big Tech analysis. Public feed carries previews; full via Stratechery Plus.

Name: Patrick Boyle On Finance
Description: A quant hedge-fund manager explains financial news, market history, and corporate blowups with dry humor and rigor. Strong on financial history, frauds, and how markets actually function.

Name: Coaching for Leaders
Description: Long-running leadership-development show interviewing authors and experts on management, influence, communication, and career growth. Practical, research-backed advice applicable to PMs leading without authority.

Name: The Pragmatic Engineer Podcast
Description: Deep dives into how software engineering works inside Big Tech and startups, with senior engineers and eng leaders. Essential for PMs who want to understand engineering culture and how to work with dev teams.

Name: The Look & Sound of Leadership
Description: An executive-coaching podcast focused on the communication, presence, and behaviors that shape how leaders are perceived. Short, tightly focused episodes ideal for PMs working on executive presence and influence.

Name: Radical Candor
Host(s): Kim Scott, Jason Rosoff & Amy Sandler
Description: Hosts unpack the Radical Candor framework — caring personally while challenging directly — through real workplace dilemmas. Strong, actionable guidance on feedback, difficult conversations, and management.

Name: Soft Skills Engineering
Description: A lighthearted Q&A show answering listener questions about the non-technical side of software careers — career growth, working with managers, teams, and communication. Useful PM-engineer empathy and collaboration.

Name: Engineering Enablement
Description: Interviews with researchers and leaders on developer productivity, engineering metrics, and developer experience. Helps PMs understand how to measure engineering effectiveness and partner with platform teams.

Name: The Engineering Leadership Podcast
Description: Conversations with engineering executives on leading teams, scaling orgs, and navigating tough leadership moments. A window into how eng leaders think about strategy, people, and cross-functional partnership.

Name: At The Table with Patrick Lencioni
Description: The author of The Five Dysfunctions of a Team applies his organizational-health and teamwork models to real leadership questions. Practical frameworks for trust, healthy conflict, and team effectiveness.

Name: HBR On Leadership
Description: Curated HBR conversations spotlighting the best leadership ideas and research from across their catalog. Concise, credible thinking on leading people, decision-making, and management.

Name: HBR IdeaCast
Description: HBR's flagship weekly interview show with leading thinkers in business and management. Broad coverage of leadership, strategy, communication, and career — an evergreen source of frameworks.

Name: Hidden Brain
Description: Explores the unconscious psychology behind human behavior, decisions, and relationships. Excellent for building intuition about user behavior, persuasion, bias, and effective collaboration.

Name: The Stack Overflow Podcast
Description: The Stack Overflow team and guests discuss software development trends, tools, and the realities of building tech. Keeps PMs current on the developer world and the concerns of the engineers they work with.

Name: Developer Tea
Description: Short, reflective episodes on the mindset, habits, and career of software developers. Quick listens that help PMs understand how engineers think about their work, growth, and decision-making.

Name: Negotiate Anything
Description: The most-downloaded negotiation podcast, teaching practical tactics for difficult conversations, persuasion, and conflict resolution. Directly useful for PMs negotiating scope, priorities, and stakeholder alignment.

Name: Coaching Real Leaders
Description: An HBR show featuring real, recorded coaching sessions with leaders navigating career and management challenges. A rare, candid look at executive coaching that PMs can learn from for their own growth.

Name: Manager Tools
Description: Highly tactical, no-nonsense guidance on the fundamentals of management — one-on-ones, feedback, delegation, and coaching. A long-running staple for anyone leading people, including PMs leading cross-functionally.

Name: Modern Mentor
Description: Bite-sized advice on workplace effectiveness, leadership, communication, and navigating office dynamics. Quick, actionable tips for leading teams and managing stakeholder relationships.

Name: ReThinking with Adam Grant
Description: Organizational psychologist Adam Grant interviews thinkers and leaders on work, motivation, and rethinking assumptions (successor to WorkLife). Sharp insights on culture, collaboration, and leadership psychology.

Name: No Stupid Questions
Description: A Freakonomics show exploring questions about human behavior, motivation, and decision-making. Builds PM judgment around psychology, habits, and how people actually behave.

Name: How to Be Awesome at Your Job
Description: Interviews packed with concrete tactics for productivity, communication, leadership, and career advancement. A practical skills-builder PMs can mine for tools to work and lead more effectively.

Name: Tech Lead Journal
Description: Interviews with tech leaders on technical leadership, engineering practices, and personal growth. Helps PMs understand the mindset and challenges of the eng leaders they partner with.

Name: Maintainable
Description: Conversations with software practitioners about working with legacy code, technical debt, and sustainable engineering practices. Gives PMs language and empathy for the tradeoffs behind tech debt.

Name: Dev Interrupted
Description: Focused on engineering leadership, developer productivity, and building high-performing dev teams. Strong for PMs who want to understand engineering metrics, delivery, and how to collaborate with eng leadership.

Name: Beyond Coding
Description: Interviews exploring software engineering, leadership, and the human side of building tech. A solid window for PMs into engineering careers, team dynamics, and technical decision-making.

Name: People Nerds (dscout)
Description: A human-insights digest from dscout's UX research blog: interviews, frameworks, ResearchOps guidance, usability and diary-study methods, and career advice for UX researchers.

Name: Boz To The Future
Description: Career and leadership lessons from Meta's CTO on building products, managing teams, and engineering culture at scale. Operator wisdom translated into reusable principles.

Name: Founder's Journal
Description: The Morning Brew co-founder shares frameworks and lessons from building businesses, from 0-to-1 to scaling. Short, practical episodes on founder decision-making and company-building.

Name: Impact Pricing
Description: Monthly newsletter plus weekly podcast from a veteran pricing expert and author of Win Keep Grow. Focuses on creating, communicating, and capturing value, with practical subscription pricing and packaging guidance.

Name: Subscription Stories
Description: Newsletter and podcast from the author of The Membership Economy, interviewing leaders (Netflix, NBA, WSJ) on subscription pricing and membership models that generate predictable recurring revenue.

Name: Future Commerce
Description: The "culture magazine for commerce"—essays, podcasts, and research at the intersection of culture and commerce, covering retail, DTC, and the future of buying.
